site.name: i23
site.description: Improve your life trajectory, one experience at a time.

probleme.title: We repeat the same experiences,
probleme.description: The stress that keeps coming back. The relationships that look the same. The energy that's always missing. The decisions you regret. It's not a lack of willpower. It's a pattern.

agitation.title: A repeated pattern becomes a trajectory
agitation.description: Every experience activates the same components: observe, predict, feel, choose, plan, act, evaluate, learn. When a single component stays negative, it colors the entire experience. And the next ones.

items.1: You see the same situation repeat
items.2: You feel the same emotional charge
items.3: You make the same default decision

solution.title: Changing one component can change the entire experience
solution.description: The i+ Method breaks down each experience into 8 steps, observed at 3 levels (self, others, environment). Each step has two poles: threat or opportunity. Transforming a single threat into an opportunity changes the outcome.

credibilite.title: 30 years of challenges, research and practice
credibilite.description: The i+ Method wasn't born from theory. It's the fruit of a lived journey: dysfunctional family, addictions, poverty, depression, chronic pain. Back to school at 34. 14 theories studied. Years of intervention and facilitation. Tested and refined continuously with hundreds of people.

masterclass.title: Understand a theme with the i+ Method

masterclass.question: Question

outils.title: Transform your threats into opportunities
outils.description: An interactive tool for each step of the i+ Method. Identify the threat, choose the opportunity, plan the action.
outils.identifyMenaceDesc: What blocks, overloads or limits you in this step of your experience?
outils.describeMenaceDesc: How does this threat affect your experience concretely?
outils.describeMenacePlaceholder: When this threat is active, here is what happens...
outils.chooseOpportuniteDesc: What would help, support or open a possibility instead?
outils.opportunitePlaceholder: Describe the opportunity you want to activate...
outils.actionPlanDesc: How will you activate this opportunity the next time the threat presents itself?
outils.actionPlanPlaceholder: Next time, here is what I will do...

accueil.title: Welcome to i23
accueil.description: A few simple questions to better understand where you are. There are no right or wrong answers.

questions.q1: In general, how rested do you feel when you wake up?
questions.q2: When something worries you, how much does it occupy your thoughts?
questions.q3: How would you describe the way you manage your time?
questions.q4: When a strong emotion appears, you tend to...
questions.q4_a: Feel it and let it pass
questions.q4_c: Push it away or ignore it
questions.q5: When you think about what truly motivates you, you...
questions.q6: In conversation, how comfortable are you being honest?
questions.q6_c: I often keep things to myself
questions.q7: How much do you trust your own judgment?
questions.q8: How would you describe your relationships right now?
questions.q9: Faced with the unexpected, your first reaction is...
questions.q9_b: I stress a bit then adjust
questions.q11: When something touches you emotionally, you...
questions.q11_b: Feel it but keep it inside
questions.q11_c: Shut it down or avoid it
questions.q12: When you need to make an important decision, you...
questions.q13: How often do you accomplish what you planned in a day?
questions.q14: How connected do you feel to the people who matter to you?
questions.q15: When you think about your future, you feel...
questions.q15_b: A mix of hope and uncertainty
questions.q16: When someone asks how you're doing, you answer...
questions.q16_c: I'm fine, even when I'm not
questions.q17: Is there something that keeps coming back in your life that you'd like to understand or change?
questions.q18: If you could improve one aspect of your life right now, what would it be?

declencheurs.title: Choose the theme
declencheurs.description: Which theme is related to the experience you want to work on?

selection.title: Select your trigger
selection.description: What activates this experience? Describe the trigger and choose the concerned sphere.
selection.placeholder: E.g.: When my colleague speaks to me in that tone...

histoire.title: Write your story
histoire.description: Describe the complete experience as if you were telling the story to a friend.
histoire.placeholder: Tell what happens, what you feel, what you do...

decortication.title: Break down your pattern
decortication.description: For each step, describe what happens in your experience. Be as precise as possible.

menaces.title: Identify your threats
menaces.description: Which of these beliefs or thoughts appear in your pattern? Select the ones that resonate.

transformation.title: Transform threats
transformation.description: You identified these threats in your pattern. The next step is to transform them into opportunities.
transformation.hint: Select the opportunities that will replace these threats.

opportunites.title: Activate your opportunities
opportunites.description: Choose the affirmations that correspond to what you want to experience instead.

nouveauSchema.title: Create your new pattern
nouveauSchema.description: Rewrite each step integrating the opportunities you selected.
nouveauSchema.placeholder: How you would like to experience this step...

preparation.title: Prepare your experiment
preparation.description: Write the new story you will live. This will be your guide when the trigger presents itself.
preparation.placeholder: When this trigger presents itself, here is what I will experience...

methode.title: The 8 steps of experience
methode.description: Every experience follows the same pattern. Understanding these steps allows you to identify what blocks and transform threats into opportunities.
methode.boucleDesc: The method works in a continuous loop: identify the trigger, break down the pattern, apply a tool, adjust the trajectory.

dashboard.title: My i23 space
dashboard.bilanDesc: A few questions to understand where you are and personalize your journey.

confidentialite.title: Privacy Policy
confidentialite.section1Content: i23 is the controller of personal data collected on the i23.ca platform. Contact: info@i23.ca.
confidentialite.section2Item2: Usage data: schemas, tools, assessments, progress (only if member chooses storage)
confidentialite.section2Item3: Payment data: processed by Stripe, never stored by i23
confidentialite.section2Item4: Browsing data: session cookies, anonymized analytics
confidentialite.section3Content: Data is used exclusively to: provide i23 services, support the member through the i+ Method, manage subscriptions, improve the platform, and comply with legal obligations.
confidentialite.section4Content: The i+ AI Coach can retain information to support the member, only with explicit consent. The member chooses from three levels:
confidentialite.section4Item1: Stored data: trajectory tracking, AI support, coach sharing if authorized
confidentialite.section4Item2: Unstored data: maximum privacy, use without tracking
confidentialite.section4Item3: Coach sharing: personalized support, schema review
confidentialite.section5Content: Data is retained while the member is subscribed, then deleted within 30 days of account deactivation, unless required by law.
confidentialite.section6Content: Data is never sold. It is shared only with: Stripe (payments), Supabase (database hosting, Canada), and OpenAI (AI, if the member uses the i+ AI Coach).
confidentialite.section7Content: In accordance with Quebec Law 25 and PIPEDA, you have the right to access, modify, delete, and port your data. To exercise these rights: info@i23.ca or use the deletion function in your account.
confidentialite.section8Content: i23 uses TLS 1.3 encryption in transit, AES-256 at rest, two-factor authentication, Row Level Security, and audit logs to protect your data.
confidentialite.section9Content: Any significant change will be notified by email. The current version is always available on this page.

outils_etape1.title: Observation tools
outils_etape1.description: These tools help you perceive and select information in a situation, distinguishing facts from interpretations.
outils_etape1.outil_1_desc: Clarify what you actually perceive in a situation.
outils_etape1.outil_2_desc: Target your attention on relevant information.
outils_etape1.outil_3_desc: Check whether your perceptions are accurate or biased.
outils_etape1.outil_4_desc: Redistribute your attention between positive and negative aspects.
outils_etape1.outil_5_desc: Spot details you tend to ignore.
outils_etape1.outil_6_desc: Identify resources and assets present in the situation.
outils_etape1.outil_7_desc: Distinguish real obstacles from perceived obstacles.
outils_etape1.outil_8_desc: Draw a complete map of the current situation.
outils_etape1.outil_9_desc: Clearly separate what is objective from what is subjective.
outils_etape1.outil_10_desc: Classify each element as fact or interpretation.

outil_scan_observation.title: Observation scan
outil_scan_observation.objectif: Clarify what you actually perceive in a situation, distinguishing facts from interpretations.
outil_scan_observation.consigne: Describe the situation, list your perceptions, then classify them. Be as honest as possible.
outil_scan_observation.s1_description: Briefly describe the situation you want to observe. Where, when, with whom, what?
outil_scan_observation.s1_exemple: My boss asked me to stay late to finish a project. He said: "We need you." I agreed, but I was frustrated.
outil_scan_observation.s2_description: What do you concretely notice? List everything you see, hear, physically feel or think.
outil_scan_observation.s2_exemple: He said "We need you" / His tone was urgent / I felt my heart race / I said yes right away
outil_scan_observation.s3_description: For each perception, determine whether it's an observable fact or a subjective interpretation.
outil_scan_observation.s3_postulat: Perceived data must be validated to avoid interpretation errors.
outil_scan_observation.s4_description: Which interpretation blocks or limits you in this situation? How does it affect your trajectory?
outil_scan_observation.s4_menacePlaceholder: E.g.: "His urgent tone means he doesn't respect my time."
outil_scan_observation.s4_impactLabel: How does this threat affect your trajectory?
outil_scan_observation.s4_impactPlaceholder: When I think that, here's what happens in my life...
outil_scan_observation.s5_description: What could you perceive with more clarity? What fact have you forgotten or minimized?
outil_scan_observation.s5_opportunitePlaceholder: E.g.: "He needs me for this specific project, not forever."
outil_scan_observation.s5_faitLabel: What fact have you forgotten or minimized?
outil_scan_observation.s5_faitPlaceholder: E.g.: "I have the right to say no. I've already delivered my tasks this week."
outil_scan_observation.s6_description: Next time this situation arises, what will you do differently?
outil_scan_observation.s6_observationPlaceholder: E.g.: "Next time, I'll observe the facts before reacting."
outil_scan_observation.s6_questionLabel: What question will you ask yourself to validate your perceptions?
outil_scan_observation.s6_questionPlaceholder: E.g.: "Is this a fact or am I interpreting?"

groupes.title: Share, experiment, progress together
groupes.description: i+ Groups are exchange and experimentation meetings on Zoom. They support repetition, adjustments and integration of the i+ Method.

accueil.title: Welcome
accueil.description: Welcoming participants, theme reminder, emotional management.

retour.title: Experimentation review
retour.description: Successes, difficulties and adjustments since the last meeting.

theorie.title: Mini theoretical content
theorie.description: Review of a step, a tool or a principle of the method.

outil.title: Using a tool
outil.description: Fill in the areas, reflect and share with the group.

discussion.title: Discussion
discussion.description: Exchanges, feedback and support among participants.

action.title: Concrete action
action.description: Each participant leaves with an action, an experiment or a strategy.

regles.r5: Participants must leave with an action.
